idea-plugin.name: Spotyy
idea-plugin.description:
Spotyy — Spotify Controls in Your IDE Status Bar

Stay in the zone. Spotyy embeds Spotify playback controls directly into the JetBrains status bar so you can manage music without ever leaving your editor.

Note: macOS only — uses AppleScript to communicate with the Spotify desktop app. The Spotify web player is not supported.

Features

  • Play / Pause — toggle playback instantly from the status bar
  • Previous & Next track — skip or rewind without leaving your IDE
  • Volume slider — click the volume icon to open an inline popup slider; auto-dismisses after 5 seconds
  • Now Playing — live artist and track name displayed right in the status bar, refreshed every 3 seconds
  • Adaptive theming — icons and colours automatically match your IDE's light or dark theme
  • Zero performance impact — all Spotify communication runs on background threads; the UI thread is never blocked

Getting Started

  1. Install Spotyy and restart your IDE.
  2. Open the Spotify desktop app on your Mac and start playing a track.
  3. Open any project — the Spotyy widget appears automatically in the bottom status bar.
  4. Use the playback buttons to control music and the volume icon to adjust level.

Requirements

  • macOS 10.15 Catalina or later
  • Spotify desktop application (installed and running)
  • Any JetBrains IDE: IntelliJ IDEA, Android Studio, PyCharm, WebStorm, CLion, GoLand, Rider, and more

Permissions

On first launch, macOS may ask for Automation permission so the IDE can control Spotify. If the widget shows no track information, open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Automation and enable Spotify access for your IDE.

Found a bug or have a feature request? Open an issue on GitHub.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.0.5

  • Broader IDE compatibility — plugin now supports IntelliJ 2022.3+ and Android Studio Giraffe and later; no longer restricted to recent builds
  • Review nudge — after 3 days of use, a one-time notification gently asks for a Marketplace review
